<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating project components**

I need to consider updating and possibly replacing parts of my review store, but it doesn't feel complete until I remove the old utilities. I'll also need to delete certain review files and make sure there are no dead imports in the App. Specifically, I should remove unused components like `VerticalBarchart.js` and a few others. There might be some legacy elements to delete as well. I should also think about how to handle the `DatePicker` component, particularly regarding its handling of currency options before they're loaded.
**Identifying potential issues**

I need to use `isOptionEqualToValue` when dealing with option objects, but since my options are strings, I might be okay there. If the options are empty and there are preset values, it could lead to displaying chips, which is something to keep in mind. There’s a potential problem with `setBaseCurrency` filtering by the same base, as `handleBaseChange` might use stale `quoteCurrencies`. I think I should check how `useEffect` initializes the date range and base/quotes during `fetchRates`. If there’s a failure in fetching currencies, it might still try to fetch rates, which could be improved with better error handling.
**Considering updates and changes**

I should probably update to the new branch, and I think that’s done now. I also need to decide on using the names `FxCharts` and `FxSummaryCards`, which sounds good. I should handle the line series by setting `showMark` to false for the other series. Updating the spinner component `src/components/Spinner/index.js` might also be necessary, and I want to make sure to build and lint the project later for any issues.
**Evaluating code improvements**

I'm thinking about inspecting `chartDataFromFx` using `sortedRates.find` in a loop, which might work. I should consider using Map to optimize things a bit more. There's also the thought that building a linter might warn me about using array filters, which could be helpful. I definitely need to update the `DatePicker` label, and I should remember to delete old files using the Delete tool.</details></think>

{
  "markdown": "Frankfurter | Free exchange rates and currency data API \n\nThis documents the new v2 API. For v1 docs, [go here](https://frankfurter.dev/v1/).\n\nFrankfurter tracks daily exchange rates from [20+ central banks and official institutions](https://frankfurter.dev/providers/), covering 150 active currencies.\n\nThe public API lives at [api.frankfurter.dev](https://api.frankfurter.dev/). No keys or usage limits. You can also [self-host](https://frankfurter.dev/deploy/).\n\n## Rates\n\nFetch the latest exchange rates.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"AUD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.6111\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"BGN\",\n    \"rate\": 1.9558\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nChange the base currency with&#96;base&#96;. Filter target currencies with&#96;quote&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?base=USD\u0026quotes=EUR,GBP */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"USD\",\n    \"quote\": \"EUR\",\n    \"rate\": 0.95294\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"USD\",\n    \"quote\": \"GBP\",\n    \"rate\": 0.79538\n  }\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Historical Rates\n\nLook up rates for a specific date.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?date=1999-01-04 */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"1999-01-04\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"GBP\",\n    \"rate\": 0.7111\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"1999-01-04\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"JPY\",\n    \"rate\": 133.73\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Time Series\n\nFetch rates over a period with&#96;from&#96; and&#96;to&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?from=2024-01-01\u0026to=2024-01-05\u0026quotes=USD */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-02\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"USD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.0956\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-03\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"USD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.0919\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip: Narrowing down currencies keeps responses small. For large date ranges, request NDJSON to stream results line by line.\n\n### Grouping\n\nDownsample a time series with&#96;group&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?from=2024-01-01\u0026group=month */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-01\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"AUD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.6263\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-01\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"BGN\",\n    \"rate\": 1.9558\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Filtering by Provider\n\nScope to specific providers with&#96;providers&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?providers=ECB */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"AUD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.6111\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"BGN\",\n    \"rate\": 1.9558\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nBy default, rates are blended across all providers. Use&#96;providers&#96; to retrieve rates from a specific source.\n\n### CSV Output\n\nRates are also available as CSV. Append&#96;.csv&#96; to the path or set the&#96;Accept&#96; header to&#96;text/csv&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates.csv */\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### NDJSON Output\n\nFor large responses, request [NDJSON](https://github.com/ndjson/ndjson-spec)(newline-delimited JSON) by setting the&#96;Accept&#96; header to&#96;application/x-ndjson&#96;. Each line is one independent JSON object, useful for streaming large time series without buffering the entire response.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ncurl -H \"Accept: application/x-ndjson\" \\\n  \"https://api.frankfurter.dev/v2/rates?from=2024-01-01\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Rate\n\nGet the rate for a single currency pair. Optionally add&#96;date&#96; or&#96;providers&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rate/EUR/USD */\n{\n  \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n  \"base\": \"EUR\",\n  \"quote\": \"USD\",\n  \"rate\": 1.0487\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Currencies\n\nGet available currencies with provider coverage.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/currencies */\n[\n  {\n    \"iso_code\": \"AUD\",\n    \"iso_numeric\": \"036\",\n    \"name\": \"Australian Dollar\",\n    \"symbol\": \"$\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"iso_code\": \"BGN\",\n    \"iso_numeric\": \"975\",\n    \"name\": \"Bulgarian Lev\",\n    \"symbol\": \"лв.\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Providers\n\nList the data sources behind the API.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/providers */\n[\n  {\n    \"key\": \"BOC\",\n    \"name\": \"Bank of Canada\",\n    \"description\": \"Daily exchange rates for...\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"key\": \"ECB\",\n    \"name\": \"European Central Bank\",\n    \"description\": \"Reference rates for...\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Conversion\n\nFetch the rate and convert in your own code.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nfunction convert(from, to, amount) {\n  fetch(&#96;https://api.frankfurter.dev/v2/rates?base=${from}\u0026quotes=${to}&#96;)\n    .then((resp) =\u003e resp.json())\n    .then((data) =\u003e {\n      const convertedAmount = (amount * data[0].rate).toFixed(2);\n      alert(&#96;${amount} ${from} = ${convertedAmount} ${to}&#96;);\n    });\n  }\n\nconvert(\"EUR\", \"USD\", 10);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nClick or tap the snippet to run it.\n\n## Self-Hosting\n\nYou can [self-host with Docker](https://frankfurter.dev/deploy/). See the deploy guide for production setup and API key configuration.\n\n## FAQ\n\nIs the API free for commercial use? Yes, absolutely. Does the API have any call limits? There are no limits. For high-volume use, consider caching responses or self-hosting. Is the v1 API being retired? No. The v1 API will continue to work. See the [v1 documentation](https://frankfurter.dev/v1/). What is the privacy policy of the API? The API itself does not collect personal data. However, the public app runs behind Cloudflare for performance, and Cloudflare will collect some basic information for analytics. This does not apply if you run the API privately. What should I do if a currency is missing? If you notice a currency missing from our data, please [open an issue](https://github.com/lineofflight/frankfurter/issues) and include a suggested source with the missing data. We're looking for non-commercial sources that publish current and historical daily rates at the end of each working day. How accurate are the rates? It depends on your use case. If you need official reference rates for reporting or compliance, filter by a specific provider. The numbers will match what that institution publishes. If you're converting prices for display, say, localizing an e-commerce site, the default blended rates work well. They're drawn from multiple sources and are accurate for practical use, though the last decimal places may shift slightly as new data comes in. Why is it called Frankfurter? Frankfurt is home to the European Central Bank, the original data source of this project.",
  "url": "https://frankfurter.dev/docs/"
}
```

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

## Done

The rewrite is implemented on the new branch `feature/frankfurter-fx-dashboard`.

The app is now a full **Frankfurter FX dashboard** instead of the old mock review dashboard. I replaced the old review store/data pipeline with a live FX flow in `src/store/useFxStore.js`, added FX chart shaping in `src/utils/chartDataFromFx.js`, KPI calculation in `src/utils/fxKpis.js`, and rewrote the UI around `src/App.js`, `src/components/DatePicker.js`, `src/components/FxSummaryCards.js`, and `src/components/FxCharts.js`.

The new dashboard includes:
- live Frankfurter data
- start/end date filters
- base currency selector
- compare-against multi-select
- KPI summary cards
- `Line`, `Bar`, `Pie`, and `Donut` tabs
- the side-label layout for pie/donut
- removal of the old details dialog, FAB, mock dataset, and review-specific helpers/components

I also updated the app branding in `public/index.html` and refined the theme in `src/index.js` for the new market-focused presentation.

## Validation

I verified:
- `npm run build` completes successfully
- the refreshed dev server compiles successfully at `http://localhost:3000/BarChartReact`
- the served app HTML now uses the new title/meta for **Frankfurter FX Dashboard**
- the served bundle contains the new FX dashboard strings like `Live Currency Markets`, `Market filters`, `Rate momentum`, and `Latest distribution snapshot`
- Frankfurter’s rates endpoint responds correctly for the selected FX use case

I also checked for stale review references in `src`, and there are none left.

## Note

The working tree already had pre-existing changes before this rewrite, so I only built on top of them and did not revert anything. I haven’t made a git commit.

If you want, the next step can be:
1. polish the visuals further for portfolio quality
2. add CSV export for the active FX range
3. add a provider selector from Frankfurter providers

{
  "markdown": "Frankfurter | Free exchange rates and currency data API \n\nThis documents the new v2 API. For v1 docs, [go here](https://frankfurter.dev/v1/).\n\nFrankfurter tracks daily exchange rates from [20+ central banks and official institutions](https://frankfurter.dev/providers/), covering 150 active currencies.\n\nThe public API lives at [api.frankfurter.dev](https://api.frankfurter.dev/). No keys or usage limits. You can also [self-host](https://frankfurter.dev/deploy/).\n\n## Rates\n\nFetch the latest exchange rates.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"AUD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.6111\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"BGN\",\n    \"rate\": 1.9558\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nChange the base currency with&#96;base&#96;. Filter target currencies with&#96;quote&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?base=USD\u0026quotes=EUR,GBP */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"USD\",\n    \"quote\": \"EUR\",\n    \"rate\": 0.95294\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"USD\",\n    \"quote\": \"GBP\",\n    \"rate\": 0.79538\n  }\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Historical Rates\n\nLook up rates for a specific date.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?date=1999-01-04 */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"1999-01-04\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"GBP\",\n    \"rate\": 0.7111\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"1999-01-04\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"JPY\",\n    \"rate\": 133.73\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Time Series\n\nFetch rates over a period with&#96;from&#96; and&#96;to&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?from=2024-01-01\u0026to=2024-01-05\u0026quotes=USD */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-02\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"USD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.0956\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-03\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"USD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.0919\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip: Narrowing down currencies keeps responses small. For large date ranges, request NDJSON to stream results line by line.\n\n### Grouping\n\nDownsample a time series with&#96;group&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?from=2024-01-01\u0026group=month */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-01\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"AUD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.6263\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-01-01\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"BGN\",\n    \"rate\": 1.9558\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Filtering by Provider\n\nScope to specific providers with&#96;providers&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates?providers=ECB */\n[\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"AUD\",\n    \"rate\": 1.6111\n  },\n  {\n    \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n    \"base\": \"EUR\",\n    \"quote\": \"BGN\",\n    \"rate\": 1.9558\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nBy default, rates are blended across all providers. Use&#96;providers&#96; to retrieve rates from a specific source.\n\n### CSV Output\n\nRates are also available as CSV. Append&#96;.csv&#96; to the path or set the&#96;Accept&#96; header to&#96;text/csv&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rates.csv */\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### NDJSON Output\n\nFor large responses, request [NDJSON](https://github.com/ndjson/ndjson-spec)(newline-delimited JSON) by setting the&#96;Accept&#96; header to&#96;application/x-ndjson&#96;. Each line is one independent JSON object, useful for streaming large time series without buffering the entire response.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ncurl -H \"Accept: application/x-ndjson\" \\\n  \"https://api.frankfurter.dev/v2/rates?from=2024-01-01\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Rate\n\nGet the rate for a single currency pair. Optionally add&#96;date&#96; or&#96;providers&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/rate/EUR/USD */\n{\n  \"date\": \"2024-11-25\",\n  \"base\": \"EUR\",\n  \"quote\": \"USD\",\n  \"rate\": 1.0487\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Currencies\n\nGet available currencies with provider coverage.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/currencies */\n[\n  {\n    \"iso_code\": \"AUD\",\n    \"iso_numeric\": \"036\",\n    \"name\": \"Australian Dollar\",\n    \"symbol\": \"$\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"iso_code\": \"BGN\",\n    \"iso_numeric\": \"975\",\n    \"name\": \"Bulgarian Lev\",\n    \"symbol\": \"лв.\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Providers\n\nList the data sources behind the API.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* /v2/providers */\n[\n  {\n    \"key\": \"BOC\",\n    \"name\": \"Bank of Canada\",\n    \"description\": \"Daily exchange rates for...\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"key\": \"ECB\",\n    \"name\": \"European Central Bank\",\n    \"description\": \"Reference rates for...\",\n    \"...\": \"...\"\n  },\n  \"...\"\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Conversion\n\nFetch the rate and convert in your own code.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nfunction convert(from, to, amount) {\n  fetch(&#96;https://api.frankfurter.dev/v2/rates?base=${from}\u0026quotes=${to}&#96;)\n    .then((resp) =\u003e resp.json())\n    .then((data) =\u003e {\n      const convertedAmount = (amount * data[0].rate).toFixed(2);\n      alert(&#96;${amount} ${from} = ${convertedAmount} ${to}&#96;);\n    });\n  }\n\nconvert(\"EUR\", \"USD\", 10);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nClick or tap the snippet to run it.\n\n## Self-Hosting\n\nYou can [self-host with Docker](https://frankfurter.dev/deploy/). See the deploy guide for production setup and API key configuration.\n\n## FAQ\n\nIs the API free for commercial use? Yes, absolutely. Does the API have any call limits? There are no limits. For high-volume use, consider caching responses or self-hosting. Is the v1 API being retired? No. The v1 API will continue to work. See the [v1 documentation](https://frankfurter.dev/v1/). What is the privacy policy of the API? The API itself does not collect personal data. However, the public app runs behind Cloudflare for performance, and Cloudflare will collect some basic information for analytics. This does not apply if you run the API privately. What should I do if a currency is missing? If you notice a currency missing from our data, please [open an issue](https://github.com/lineofflight/frankfurter/issues) and include a suggested source with the missing data. We're looking for non-commercial sources that publish current and historical daily rates at the end of each working day. How accurate are the rates? It depends on your use case. If you need official reference rates for reporting or compliance, filter by a specific provider. The numbers will match what that institution publishes. If you're converting prices for display, say, localizing an e-commerce site, the default blended rates work well. They're drawn from multiple sources and are accurate for practical use, though the last decimal places may shift slightly as new data comes in. Why is it called Frankfurter? Frankfurt is home to the European Central Bank, the original data source of this project.",
  "url": "https://frankfurter.dev/docs/"
}
```

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


## Can you turn this into a gold tracker?

**Yes, at the UI level.** Your app is already a “time-series + comparison + snapshot” dashboard: tabs, date range, Zustand store, and chart mappers. A gold tracker is the same idea with **different data** (gold price in USD/oz, EUR/oz, etc.) and **labels** (not “USD/EUR pairs”).

What you’d change in practice:

- **Replace** Frankfurter calls (`api.frankfurter.dev`) with a **gold/metals API** (or a historical series API).
- **Reshape** the response in a small adapter (like your `chartDataFromFx` pattern) into `{ dates[], values[] }` for a **line** chart, and optional aggregates for **bar** / **pie** (pie is not a natural fit for “single asset price over time”; it’s better for **allocation** or **period comparisons** if you design it that way).
- **Drop or rework** FX-specific concepts: base/quote currency baskets, Frankfurter “providers”, etc., unless the API supports comparable filters.

**Frankfurter is not a gold API** — it’s for fiat exchange rates. You can keep the same MUI chart components; you cannot get real gold spot history from Frankfurter.

---

## Best *free* API for gold?

There isn’t one universal “best” — it depends on **live spot vs historical**, **rate limits**, **API key**, and **CORS** (many keys + server-only).

Common choices people use for **portfolio demos**:

| Option | Role | Notes |
|--------|------|--------|
| **[FRED](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/)** (St. Louis Fed) | **Historical gold series** (e.g. LBMA / related series) | Free API key; **usually not callable from the browser** (CORS) — use a small backend or serverless proxy. Great for **line charts** over years. |
| **Metals / commodity APIs with a free tier** (e.g. metals-api style providers) | **Spot or daily metals** | Often **API key + monthly cap**; check current docs and **CORS**; good for a **simple gold price + chart**. |
| **Alpha Vantage** (commodity endpoints) | **Daily/intraday-style demos** | Free key; **strict rate limits**; verify commodity coverage and terms. |

**Practical recommendation for a React SPA:**

1. If you want **minimum backend work** and **browser `fetch`**: pick a provider that **documents CORS** or use their **official client pattern**; many still require a **proxy** to hide the key.
2. If you want **reliable historical charts** and **no vendor surprises**: **FRED + tiny serverless function** (or Express route) is a very standard portfolio pattern.

---

## Short answer

- **Yes**, you can convert the dashboard into a gold tracker by swapping the data layer and copy; the chart shell can stay.
- **Best “free” depends on your constraint**:  
  - **Historical + academic credibility** → **FRED** (often via proxy).  
  - **Simple spot/daily with a key** → a **metals API with a free tier** (check their 2026 pricing/CORS docs before you commit).

If you tell me whether you need **live spot**, **daily close only**, or **long history**, and whether you’re OK adding a **small proxy**, I can narrow this to one API and a matching chart shape. I’m in **Ask mode** — I can’t change the repo for you; switch to **Agent mode** if you want implementation.

**Analyzing path joining**

I discovered that `path.posix.join('/BarChartReact', '/api/fred/series/observations')` in Node 18+ behaves differently than expected, causing the path to be joined incorrectly. The 302 redirect causes the proxy to miss the path prefix, leading to history fallback returning index.html instead of the API response. 

**Adjusting proxy paths**

I'm updating setupProxy.js to register the proxy on both `/api/fred` and `/BarChartReact/api/fred` paths. I'm adding a second proxy middleware for the public path version of the API endpoint. I'm duplicating the proxy config for the public path version of the API endpoint. I'm updating the proxy configuration to handle FRED API endpoint paths correctly. I'm refining the path rewriting logic to consistently strip leading segments and normalize API paths to `/fred/series/observations`. I'm implementing path rewriting logic to normalize API paths by stripping leading segments and mapping to `/fred/series/observations`. I'm testing regex patterns to match and rewrite API paths, focusing on optional leading segments. I've confirmed the regex pattern correctly rewrites `/api/fred/series` to `/fred/series` and will implement the path rewrite in the proxy config. I'm implementing the proxy path rewrite and updating useGoldStore to handle PUBLIC_URL correctly for development vs production environments. _**Agent (composer-2)**_

Identifying the issue: with `homepage` set to GitHub Pages, CRA sets `PUBLIC_URL` to `/BarChartReact`. Requests to `/api/fred` don't start with that path, so `redirectServedPathMiddleware` redirects to `/BarChartReact/api/fred/...`. The proxy only mounts on `/api/fred`, so the redirected request isn't proxied and the dev server returns `index.html` — causing the JSON parse error. Fixing by mounting the FRED proxy on both `/api/fred` and `${homepage}/api/fred`, and building the client fetch URL with `PUBLIC_URL` so requests hit the proxy directly.

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
With **`homepage`** set to your GitHub Pages URL, CRA sets **`PUBLIC_URL`** to `/BarChartReact` in dev. The dev server’s **`redirectServedPath`** middleware sends any request that does **not** start with `/BarChartReact` to a URL under `/BarChartReact`. So a fetch to **`/api/fred/...`** was redirected to **`/BarChartReact/api/fred/...`**.

The proxy was only mounted on **`/api/fred`**, so the **redirected** request never hit the proxy and fell through to the SPA fallback → **`index.html`** → `response.json()` failed with **Unexpected token '<'**.

### Fixes
1. **`src/setupProxy.js`** – Register the same FRED proxy on:
   - `/api/fred`
   - **`/BarChartReact/api/fred`** (pathname taken from `package.json` `homepage`, same idea as CRA)

   **`pathRewrite`** now supports both `/api/fred/...` and `/BarChartReact/api/fred/...`.

2. **`src/store/useGoldStore.js`** – In development, build the base as **`${PUBLIC_URL}/api/fred`** when `PUBLIC_URL` is set (e.g. `/BarChartReact/api/fred`), so the first request goes straight to a proxied path and avoids the redirect + HTML response.

3. **Parsing** – If the body looks like HTML (`<`), show a clear error instead of a JSON parse exception.

Restart **`npm start`** so `setupProxy` reloads, keep **`FRED_API_KEY`** in **`.env.local`**, and open the app at **`http://localhost:3000/BarChartReact/`** (or the root URL CRA prints). Data loads should work now.
